audit_items.proof_trace: All memory management in db.c is implemented via Redis's reference counting (incrRefCount/decrRefCount) with a consistent lazyfree null-sentinel pattern: in dbOverwrite, freeObjAsync(old) schedules async release, then dictSetVal(&auxentry, NULL) zeroes the aux copy before dictFreeVal is called; dictObjectDestructor (server.c:1171) guards against NULL with 'if (val == NULL) return', preventing any double-free or UAF. Callers of dbUnshareStringValue always capture the return value (confirmed via grep), renameGenericCommand increments refcount before deleting source, and moveCommand increments refcount before dbDelete — no pointer is dereferenced after being freed.
